sourceMovie.title: Wonka
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Wonka (2023) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul King's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Puss in Boots: The Last Wish
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wonka clicked for you, Puss in Boots: The Last Wish (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wonka clicked for you, Enchanted (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wonka?
faq.answer: Try Puss in Boots: The Last Wish, Enchanted, The Croods: A New Age—each shares DNA with Wonka while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wonka?
faq.answer: Wonka (2023) is best described as Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wonka part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wonka Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
